name: Chickpeas

book_claims.claim: Far lower calorie density than cheese while providing fibre and protein
book_claims.mechanism: 100g cooked chickpeas = 164 cal (2.6g fat, 27g carbs incl 7g fibre, 9g protein) vs 100g Parmesan = 384 cal (28g fat, 0g fibre, 33g protein); fibre induces early satiety

book_claims.claim: Concentrated source of protein, fibre, vitamins, minerals and low-glycaemic starches
book_claims.mechanism: Complex carbohydrates with high amylose content slowly digested, low glycaemic index

supplementary_research.finding: A 2010 RCT (Appetite, PMID 20226994) found that participants consuming chickpeas reported greater satiety, reduced overall food intake, and lower desire to eat processed snacks compared to white bread controls.

A 2016 systematic review (Nutrients, PMID 27012573) found chickpea consumption was associated with improved glycemic control, reduced serum cholesterol, improved weight management, and beneficial effects on gut microbiota.

A 2012 RCT (J Food Sci Technol, PMID 22916806) found that adding chickpeas to the diet significantly increased dietary fiber intake and improved lipid profiles within 12 weeks, with reduced total and LDL cholesterol.
A 2014 CMAJ meta-analysis (PMID 24871476) of 21 RCTs found that one daily serving of pulses including chickpeas reduced LDL cholesterol by 5%, contributing to cardiovascular risk reduction.

culinary_pairings.source: Hummus -- sesame adds methionine complementing chickpea's lysine for complete protein
Pasta e ceci -- complete protein; traditional Neapolitan comfort food
Essential spice in falafel, chana masala, and hummus
Acid brightens earthy flavors; vitamin C improves iron absorption
Finishing fat for hummus and roasted chickpeas
Aromatic foundation of hummus, chana, and stews
Chana palak; iron from both sources enhanced by combined vitamin C

seasonality: Dried chickpeas available year-round. Canned are convenient and nutritionally similar. Fresh green chickpeas available briefly in spring in Mediterranean climates. Soak dried chickpeas 24 hours before cooking for best results.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: High in both soluble and insoluble fiber plus resistant starch. One cup provides 12g fiber -- 40% of daily needs.
High in lysine; combine with grains or sesame for complete amino acid profile. One cup provides 15g protein.
Excellent source for DNA methylation and cardiovascular health. One cup provides 71% RDA.
Cofactor for antioxidant enzymes. Partially bound by phytic acid; soaking and cooking reduce phytate levels.
Important for iron metabolism and connective tissue formation.

synergies.description: Complete protein with all essential amino acids; traditional Italian combination
Combined in hummus for complete amino acid profile and healthy fats
Vitamin C from lemon improves non-heme iron absorption
